obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Regulation 9(4) of the
Trading Licence Regulations 2020 provides as under:
“(4) The Trading Licensee shall make an appropriate application accompanied by
prescribed fees for up-gradation of its licence to a higher category or down-gradation
of its licence to a lower category in order to fulfil the c...

Regulation 15(3) of the Trading Licence Regulations 2020 provides as under:
“15(3) Down gradation of licence:
(a) The Trading Licensee may file an application to the Commission for down
gradation of its licence to a lower category at any time:
Provided that the Trading Licensee shall not be allowed ...

(b) The Trading Licensee shall be required to upload the audited special balance sheet
to support the compliance of New Worth requirement for lower category licence.

(c) While applying for down gradation of licence, the Trading Licensee shall be required
to pay the licence fee applicable for the category of licence for which the application is
being submitted.”
8.

On perusal of the
application, we observe that the Petitioner, being an existing trading licensee, fulfils all
technical requirements for the grant of a Category ‘V’ inter-State trading licence
specified in Regulation 3(2) of the Trading Licence Regulations 2020.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
As regards the
net worth requirements for Category ‘V’, Regulation 3 (3) (a) of the Trading Licence
Regulations, 2020 provides that a person applying for a Category ‘V’ trading licence
should have a net worth of Rs. 2 crore and should have maintained minimum current
ratio and liquidity ratio of 1:1 ...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The
licensee shall remain bound by the terms and conditions contained in the order dated
21.6.2012 in Petition No. 115/TDL/2012, whereby it was issued with Category ‘I’
trading licence under Trading Licence Regulations 2009 and the terms and conditions
specified in the Trading Licence Regulations, 2...

Let an extract copy of this order be sent to the Central Government (Ministry of
Power) and the Central Electricity Authority in terms of sub-section (7) of Section 15
of the Electricity Act, 2003, for their information and record.
